content: Just as the pedagogical definition of reflexion frames it as a deliberate step students take to connect new learned vocabulary to their existing lived knowledge to deepen long-term retention, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to explicitly mirror this language learning practice: instead of only contrasting inconsistent solution perspectives, LLMs should be prompted to map their prior self-generated outputs to new problem-solving feedback the same way students map new vocabulary terms to contextual personal experience. This cross-pollination creates a more robust, contextually grounded self-reflection pipeline for autonomous agents.
